description_long: This object is a classic portable radio receiver with a rectangular body and a prominent carrying handle on top. The front face features a large circular speaker grille, a series of control knobs, and a grid of buttons, along with a horizontal frequency indicator window. A long, thin metallic antenna extends from the top, angled for optimal signal reception. The casing appears to be made of dark plastic, with metallic accents on the handle and antenna. The back and sides are plain, with the main interactive elements concentrated on the front. The overall design is utilitarian, evoking mid-to-late 20th-century portable radios commonly found in urban environments.
description: A rectangular, portable radio receiver with a large speaker grille, control knobs, buttons, and a long metallic antenna. The body is dark plastic with metallic accents and a prominent carrying handle.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thickness of the radio, the protruding control knob, and the extended antenna.
description_view_1: Rear view displaying the plain back panel, the full length of the handle, and the angled antenna.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the handle and antenna, with minimal visible controls.
description_view_3: Front view revealing the speaker grille, control knobs, button grid, and the frequency indicator window.
